slides.headline: Before you send your next sync pitch, run this audit.
slides.eyebrow: Education · The composer audit: 6 things to fix before you pitch
fit_warnings: [01 hook.eyebrow] copy (64 chars) exceeds slot budget (48 at 44px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body: Most composers who fail at the brief stage don't fail on music quality.
They fail on one of these 6 things. ↓

/ RIGHTS CHAIN DOCUMENTATION
Can you confirm in writing who owns the master and who controls publishing?
Not "probably me." Confirmed and documented.
If not: fix this before anything else.

/ SAMPLE CLEARANCE
Does every track you're pitching use 100% original material?
One uncleared sample in your best track = that track is unplaceable.
If you're unsure: assume it's not cleared.

/ FILE QUALITY
Do you have broadcast-spec WAV files for every track you're pitching?
Not 320k MP3. Not a Spotify link.
Ready to send today. / STEMS
When a supervisor asks "can I get stems?" — do you have them?
Not "I'll re-export from the session."
Organised, named, cloud-accessible within 10 minutes. / METADATA
Is your ISRC embedded in the file?
Are BPM, key, and mood tags complete?
Is your PRO registration current and reflected in the metadata? / RESPONSE TIME
Can you respond to a brief within 24 hours?
Not "I'll check when I see it."
An active notification. A clear process. A fast reply. Supervisors give the placement to whoever responds first with the right track. Not the best track. The right track, first. Audit now. Fix the gaps before the brief lands. #SyncAudit #AfricanComposers #SyncMaster #MusicBusiness
